evals.prompt: Please review this PR. It adds a retry loop around payment submission, changes the order status update, and touches the webhook handler. I care most about whether anything here could double-charge or leave orders stuck.
evals.expected_output: Review focuses first on correctness, state consistency, retries, idempotency, and webhook safety, with evidence-based findings rather than style nits.

evals.prompt: Can you audit these changes? The team refactored a React + TypeScript settings page and moved some validation from the form into a shared helper. Please call out anything risky or likely to regress.
evals.expected_output: Review checks behavior preservation, validation movement, React/TypeScript concerns when applicable, and prioritizes user-visible regressions and maintainability.

evals.prompt: I do not need a line-by-line nitpick. I want the top issues in this backend diff: a migration, a new cache layer, and some logging changes around account deletion.
evals.expected_output: Review stays selective, covers migration safety, cache coherence, privacy/logging risk, and returns a concise high-signal findings list.
